forecasts.reasoning: The question asks if a streak of the exact word "tariff" or "tariffs" appearing on the specified NYT or WSJ URLs will be broken at least once over a 42-day period (May 20 to June 30, 2025). This is incredibly likely to resolve YES. First, maintaining a 42-day continuous streak of any specific news keyword without a single miss on either paper is historically very difficult due to shifting news cycles, weekends, and the use of synonyms like "levies", "duties", or "taxes". Second, the WSJ does not publish a Sunday print edition, likely resulting in a 404 or empty page for the WSJ resolution URL on all six Sundays in the period. This means on those six Sundays, only the NYT needs to omit the word to satisfy the criteria. The Sunday NYT is heavy on feature articles and its business section focuses on longer-form pieces rather than daily trade updates, making it highly probable that at least one of those Sundays (or another slow news day, like Memorial Day or Juneteenth) will lack the word "tariff" in its headlines and blurbs. Consequently, there are multiple highly probable opportunities for the word to vanish from both pages simultaneously. Allowing for minor unforeseen caveats (e.g. constant "tariff tracker" widgets), the probability points solidly to YES.
